LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF PAULA RYAN'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND WRIT OF ATTACHMENT AS TO AISLING FERGUSON IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS MET THE REQUIREMENTS SET OUT IN CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 483.010 BECAUSE THE CLAIM IS BASED ON A CONTRACT, THE AMOUNT SOUGHT IS OF A FIXED OR READILY ASCERTAINABLE SUM, AND THE WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS NOT SECURED BY AN INTEREST IN REAL PROPERTY. BOND TO BE POSTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $10,000.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR FULL TEXT.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG).

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-13-2016 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Set For Trial On Jul-05-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment - Aisling Ferguson - Filed By Plaintiff
Off Calendar Nov-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David E. Harris of Miller Starr Regalia telephone number 925-935-9400 for Plaintiff.
E. Page Allinson of American Mortgage Law Group, P.C. telephone number 415-878-0030 for Defendants.

Ruling: The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PAULA RYAN'S Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment as to Aisling Ferguson is granted. Plaintiff has met the requirements set out in Code of Civil Procedure section 483.010 because the claim is based on a contract, the amount sought is of a fixed or readily ascertainable sum, and the writ of attachment is not secured by an interest in real property. Plaintiff has also demonstrated the probable validity of the underlying claim. Notably, Defendants were in breach of the agreement prior to the CFPB investigation and the issuance of the consent order. Both insolvency and the inability to otherwise pay damages are appropriate measures of irreparable harm that might render an arbitration award ineffectual, as would support issuance of writ of attachment as a provisional remedy pending arbitration. (See California Retail Portfolio Fund GMBH & Co. KG v. Hopkins Real Estate Group (2001) 193 Cal.App.4th 849.) Plaintiff has submitted sufficient evidence to show that Defendants are insolvent and would not be able to pay an arbitration award. (Ryan Declaration Exhibit B.) Plaintiff's failure to file an undertaking is not
fatal because a plain reading of the code section indicates that the undertaking must be filed before the writ is issued. (Code of Civil Procedure section 489.210.) Defendants' argument that Plaintiff has not met the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 438.010(c) is unavailing because that section prohibits attachment on real property used primarily for personal, family, or household purposes and the present case does not involve real property used for personal purposes. Bond to be posted in the amount of $10,000.00. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment - Robert Seifert - By Plaintiff
Off Calendar Nov-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David E. Harris of Miller Starr Regalia telephone number 925-935-9400 for Plaintiff.
E. Page Allinson of American Mortgage Law Group, P.C. telephone number 415-878-0030 for Defendants.

Ruling: The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PAULA RYAN'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment as to Robert Seifert is granted. Plaintiff has met the requirements set out in Code of Civil Procedure section 483.010 because the claim is based on a contract, the amount sought is of a fixed or readily ascertainable sum, and the writ of attachment is not secured by an interest in real property. Plaintiff has also demonstrated the probable validity of the underlying claim. Notably, Defendants were in breach of the agreement prior to the CFPB investigation and the issuance of the consent order. Both insolvency and the inability to otherwise pay damages are appropriate measures of irreparable harm that might render an arbitration award ineffectual, as would support issuance of writ of attachment as a provisional remedy pending arbitration. (See California Retail Portfolio Fund GMBH & Co. KG v. Hopkins Real Estate Group (2001) 193 Cal.App.4th 849.) Plaintiff has submitted sufficient evidence to show that Defendants are insolvent and would not be able to pay an arbitration award. (Ryan Declaration Exhibit B.) Plaintiff's failure to file an undertaking is not fatal because a plain reading of the code section indicates that the undertaking must be filed before the writ is issued. (Code of Civil Procedure section 489.210.) Defendants' argument that Plaintiff has not met the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 438.010(c) is unavailing because that section prohibits attachment on real property used primarily for personal, family, or household purposes and the present case does not involve real property used for personal purposes. Bond to be posted in the amount of $10,000.00. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)
